votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking permutation equivalence, equality of elements at indices not divisible by 3, and sortedness of elements at indices divisible by 3.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking permutation via sorting, equality at indices not divisible by 3, and sortedness of elements at indices divisible by 3.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks permutation by comparing sorted lists, (2) verifies non-divisible-by-3 indices are unchanged, and (3) checks that elements at indices divisible by 3 are sorted. The length check is implicit in the permutation check via sorted comparison. All concrete behaviors match the Coq specification.

The length check is implied by the permutation check in Coq. All concrete behaviors match.

Both require the output to be a permutation of input, keep all indices not divisible by 3 unchanged, and ensure elements at indices divisible by 3 are non-decreasing. Length handling and modulus semantics align, so concrete behavior matches.
